Based on the SEC EDGAR Prospectus Supplement for the Republic of Panama's 4.500% Global Bonds due 2047 (ISIN: US698299BG85), here are the specific details requested:

**A. DAY-COUNT EVIDENCE**
**A1.** "For the 2047 bonds: 4.500% per annum, computed on the basis of a 360-day year, consisting of twelve 30-day months."
**A2.** This sentence appears in the summary table under the heading **"The Global Bonds"** (specifically in the "Interest Rate" row) and on the cover page of the prospectus supplement.
**A3.** BOND_BASIS_30_360
**A4.** **Inferred.** The prospectus explicitly dictates the "360-day year, consisting of twelve 30-day months" math, which is the definition of the 30/360 day-count convention. However, the exact sub-variant (e.g., US 30/360 vs. ISMA 30/360) is not pinpointed by name in the text and is inferred based on standard US market conventions for SEC-registered sovereign global bonds.

**B. PRINCIPAL REPAYMENT**
**B1.** Scheduled installments.
**B2.** The principal is repaid in three equal annual installments (33.33% of the original principal each):
*   May 15, 2045 (33.33%)
*   May 15, 2046 (33.33%)
*   May 15, 2047 (33.33%)
**B3.** "Panama will pay the principal of the 2047 bonds in three equal annual installments on May 15 of each year, commencing on May 15, 2045." (Found on the **Cover Page** and in **"The Global Bonds"** summary section under "Amortization of Principal").

**C3.** **Make-Whole Call:** 25 basis points. 
*Verbatim phrase:* "Prior to November 15, 2046 (six months prior to the maturity date of the 2047 bonds), the 2047 bonds will be redeemable, in whole or in part, at any time and from time to time, at Panama's option, on not less than 30 nor more than 60 days' notice, at a redemption price equal to the greater of (1) 100% of the principal amount of the 2047 bonds being redeemed and (2) the sum of the present values of the remaining scheduled payments of principal and interest on the 2047 bonds being redeemed (excluding the portion of any such interest accrued to the redemption date) discounted to the redemption date on a semi-annual basis (assuming a 360-day year consisting of twelve 30-day months) at the Treasury Yield (as defined below), plus 25 basis points, plus accrued and unpaid interest and additional amounts, if any, to, but excluding, the redemption date."
